LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION. (CCP 430.10(E)). SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION (CCP 430.10(E)), IN LIGHT OF RULING ON FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. WITHOUT A CONTRACT THERE CAN BE NO BREACH OF AN IMPLIED COVENANT. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION (CCP 430.10(E)) AND IS NOT PLED WITH PARTICULARITY. IF PLAINTIFF FILES A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, ALL OF THE OPERATIVE ALLEGATIONS SHOULD BE IN THAT PLEADING; THE COURT SHOULD NOT NEED TO REFER TO PRIOR DECLARATIONS TO UNDERSTAND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. Off Calendar May-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Brad D. Sanders, Esq. of Law Offices of Brad D. Sanders 221-2090 pltf & opposing party in propria persona (appearing via CourtCall); Michael B. Tannatt, Esq. of AlvaradoSmith APC 213/229-2400 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer to First Amended Complaint: Sustain with leave to amend as to all causes of action. First cause of action for breach of contract does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (CCP 430.10(e)). Second cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action (CCP 430.10(e)), in light of ruling on first cause of action. Without a contract there can be no breach of an implied covenant. Third cause of action for fraud does not state facts sufficient to state a cause of action (CCP 430.10(e)) and is not pled with particularity. If plaintiff files a Second Amended Complaint, all of the operative allegations should be in that pleading; the Court should not need to refer to prior declarations to understand plaintiff's claims. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
